text.headline: Persian Constitutional Revolution Begins
text.text: Widespread protests against the Qajar monarchy lead to the establishment of Iran's first parliament (Majlis) and a constitution. Wikipedia

text.headline: Discovery of Oil
text.text: The first major oil strike in the Middle East occurs in Masjed Soleyman, fundamentally changing Iran's economic and political future. Wikipedia

text.headline: 1921 Coup d'état
text.text: Reza Khan and Zia'eddin Tabatabaee seize power in a coup, paving the way for the end of the Qajar dynasty. Wikipedia

text.headline: Rise of the Pahlavi Dynasty
text.text: Reza Khan is crowned Reza Shah Pahlavi, beginning a period of rapid modernization and secularization. Wikipedia

text.headline: Anglo-Soviet Invasion of Iran
text.text: Allied forces invade Iran during WWII to secure oil supply routes. Reza Shah is forced to abdicate in favor of his son. Wikipedia

text.headline: 1953 Coup d'état (Operation Ajax)
text.text: A CIA-backed coup overthrows the democratically elected Prime Minister Mohammad Mossadegh after he nationalized the oil industry. Wikipedia

text.headline: 15 Khordad Uprising
text.text: Protests against the Shah's White Revolution lead to the arrest of Ayatollah Khomeini and a violent crackdown. Wikipedia

text.headline: Islamic Revolution
text.text: The Pahlavi dynasty falls, and the Islamic Republic of Iran is established under Ayatollah Ruhollah Khomeini. Wikipedia

text.headline: Iran Hostage Crisis Begins
text.text: Iranian students seize the U.S. Embassy in Tehran, holding 52 Americans hostage for 444 days. Wikipedia

text.headline: Iran-Iraq War Begins
text.text: Iraq invades Iran, starting a brutal eight-year conflict that remains one of the deadliest wars in modern history. Wikipedia

text.headline: Death of Ayatollah Khomeini
text.text: The founder of the Islamic Republic dies; Ali Khamenei is subsequently appointed the new Supreme Leader. Wikipedia

text.headline: Election of Mohammad Khatami
text.text: A reformist landslide victory signals a period of attempted social and political liberalization. Wikipedia

text.headline: Green Movement Protests
text.text: Massive protests erupt following the disputed presidential election results, the largest civil unrest since 1979. Wikipedia

text.headline: JCPOA Nuclear Deal Signed
text.text: Iran and world powers reach a landmark agreement to limit Iran's nuclear program in exchange for sanctions relief. Wikipedia

text.headline: Assassination of Qasem Soleimani
text.text: A U.S. drone strike kills Iran's top military commander in Baghdad, leading to a major spike in regional tensions. Wikipedia

text.headline: Death of Mahsa Amini
text.text: The death of a young woman in morality police custody sparks the 'Woman, Life, Freedom' nationwide protest movement. Wikipedia

text.headline: Death of Ebrahim Raisi
text.text: President Ebrahim Raisi and Foreign Minister Hossein Amir-Abdollahian die in a helicopter crash in East Azerbaijan. Wikipedia
